name: Prose: "The Wind on Haunted Hill" — Ruskin Bond

sourceRefs.label: civilserviceaspirants.in - two dedicated MCQ sets (12 questions total), Group 2/2A specific
Ruskin Bond honoured with the Sahitya Akademi Award (cross-referenced from the already-shipped 'I Can't Climb Trees Anymore' topic)

researchNotes: OCR index search for 'haunted hill' returned zero hits — web research only, but a clean, internally consistent 12-question set across 2 source pages. A third Ruskin Bond piece in this syllabus (alongside 'I Can't Climb Trees Anymore' and 'When the Trees Walked') — this one a gentle ghost-adjacent story set in the Himalayan hills, where the supposed 'haunting' is treated matter-of-factly rather than as genuine horror.

reading.intro: Set in the Himalayan hills, this story follows Usha, a brave and practical girl unafraid of the local 'haunted' ruins, on a day that begins with household chores and a shopping trip across the hill to the bazaar.

qa.question: Where is the story set, and what is the name of the central girl character?
qa.answer: In the Himalayan hills. The girl's name is Usha.

qa.question: What were the children doing at the beginning of the story, and what was used to hold the clothes down?
qa.answer: Spreading clothes out to dry. Stones were used to hold the clothes down (presumably against the hill wind).

qa.question: Who was always eager to visit the bazaar, and why did he NOT go this time?
qa.answer: Suresh was always ready to visit the bazaar — but this time he couldn't go because he had to chop wood.

qa.question: Why was Usha not afraid of ghosts, despite the hill's reputation for being haunted?
qa.answer: Because she believed ghosts don't come out during the daytime — a simple, practical reasoning rather than any special charm or protection.

qa.question: Where did Usha go shopping, what did she buy for herself, and what colour was it?
qa.answer: She went to the bazaar across the hill. She bought herself a necklace, coloured AMBER.

qa.question: What did Usha get repaired at the cobbler's, and what bird sang in the ruins?
qa.answer: She got her MOTHER's slippers repaired at the cobbler's (not her own). A Whistling Thrush sang in the ruins.

questions.prompt: "The Wind on Haunted Hill" is set in:
What is the name of the central girl character in the story?
What were the children doing at the start of the story?
What was used to hold the drying clothes down?
Why didn't Suresh go to the bazaar this time, even though he usually loved to?
Why was Usha not afraid of ghosts?
options: She had a dog with her
Ghosts don't come out in the daytime
She believed she could fly away
questions.explanation: Ghosts don't come out in the daytime.

questions.prompt: What did Usha buy for herself at the bazaar, and what colour was it?
What did Usha get repaired at the cobbler's?
What bird sang in the ruins on the hill?
